title.zh: FujiDay 101 — 富士胶片调色入门

description.en: Learn Fujifilm photo grading with FujiDay: film simulations, recipes, Alex Webb composition, and chaining workflows. For photographers and AI agents who want to apply authentic Fujifilm looks to digital images.
description.zh: 学习使用 FujiDay 进行富士胶片风格调色：胶片模拟、配方生成、Alex Webb 构图、链式工作流。面向想要给数码照片赋予纯正富士味道的摄影师和 AI agent。

learningOutcomes: Understand what FujiDay is and how it maps Fujifilm film simulations to digital image processing
Identify all 7 classic Fujifilm film simulations and know when to use each
Generate structured FujiDay recipes with proper parameter adjustments
Apply Alex Webb composition technique using FujiDay's cropping tools
Chain composition analysis into Fujifilm grading workflow
Export production-ready graded images in multiple formats

modules.learningObjective: Understand FujiDay's purpose: bringing Fujifilm film simulation aesthetics to any image via AI.
modules.passSignal: Learner can explain what a 'film simulation' is and why FujiDay exists.

modules.learningObjective: Memorize and distinguish all 7 Fujifilm film simulations: ACROS, Classic Chrome, Classic Neg., ETERNA, ASTIA, PROVIA, Velvia.
modules.passSignal: Learner can match a given photography scenario to the best film simulation without prompting.

modules.learningObjective: Understand all recipe parameters: Dynamic Range, Highlight, Shadow, Color, Grain, WB Shift, Color Chrome.
modules.passSignal: Learner can read a FujiDay recipe and explain what each parameter does in plain language.

modules.learningObjective: Understand Alex Webb's signature style: high contrast, saturated colors, edge tension, layered composition.
modules.passSignal: Learner can identify whether a given image suits Webb-style cropping and explain why.

title.zh: 构图到调色的链式工作流

modules.learningObjective: Master the FujiDay composition-first then grading workflow: analyze → crop → grade → export.
modules.passSignal: Learner can execute a full composition + grading chain on a sample image.

manifest.sensitiveDataHandling: This course processes user-provided images locally. No data leaves the machine.
